content: PyInstaller + pygame gotchas: (1) --onefile mode may drop non-.py assets (fonts, sounds) unless explicitly added via --add-data; (2) frozen app's working directory becomes a temp extraction dir (sys._MEIPASS) -- code must resolve asset paths via sys._MEIPASS/base_path helper, not relative-to-cwd paths; (3) PyInstaller flattens folder structure in some modes, breaking asset sub-folder references unless the .spec file explicitly preserves structure; (4) onefile startup is slower (self-extracts every launch) -- onedir is recommended for iteration/debugging; (5) libraries with external C data/DLLs (e.g. pyproj's PROJ data files) require manual PyInstaller hooks -- this is a strong argument for preferring geographiclib (pure Python, no data files) to sidestep the issue entirely, decided now even though packaging itself is a later phase.
